articles.title: New York City’s Higher-End Retail Deserts, and How to Fill Them
articles.narrative: This article explores the phenomenon of "retail deserts" in affluent New York City neighborhoods like Windsor Terrace and Kensington, where residents have significant disposable income but a dearth of desirable retail options. The author argues that proximity to established, upscale retail corridors in adjacent neighborhoods, coupled with retailers' preference for co-tenancy with similar brands, creates a self-perpetuating cycle of limited retail development. Zoning requirements, or the lack thereof, for ground-floor retail in residential buildings also play a crucial role in shaping the retail landscape, as evidenced by the contrast between older residential areas and newly rezoned districts like Gowanus.

articles.title: Gigi Hadid’s Guest in Residence Brand Leases Second NYC Store at 1061 Madison Avenue
articles.narrative: Gigi Hadid's luxury cashmere brand, Guest in Residence, has secured a 3,550-square-foot retail lease at 1061 Madison Avenue on Manhattan's Upper East Side. This marks the brand's second New York City location, expanding its brick-and-mortar presence. The lease, finalized in February, includes ground-floor, second-floor, and basement space, with an asking rent of $750,000 annually.

data_points.address: 1061 Madison Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Located one block from Central Park and the Metropolitan Museum of Art.
data_points.project_notes: The space was previously occupied by activewear retailer Bandier.

articles.title: New TSCG President Nichole Popovics on What to Expect at ICSC
articles.narrative: Nichole Popovics, the newly appointed president of TSCG, a retail-focused real estate brokerage, shared her expectations and advice for attendees at ICSC. Popovics, who has extensive experience in the retail sector, emphasized the importance of gauging the market's sentiment, noting a mix of optimism and caution among clients. She highlighted the value of networking and encouraged first-time attendees to be observant, ask questions, and not be intimidated by industry veterans, as unexpected connections can lead to significant opportunities.

articles.title: Tech Firm Profound Inks 30K-SF Deal at GFP’s 5-9 Union Square West
articles.narrative: Marketing intelligence platform Profound has secured a 30,000-square-foot lease at GFP Real Estate's 5-9 Union Square West, also known as the Spingler Building. The deal encompasses two full floors in the eight-story office property, which overlooks Union Square Park. This new location marks Profound's second office on Union Square West, expanding its presence in the area.

data_points.address: 5-9 Union Square West, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Eight-story office building overlooking Union Square Park.
data_points.project_notes: Space previously occupied by architect and designer David Rockwell.

articles.title: Accounting Firm Prosperity Partners Signs 24K-SF Lease at 575 Madison Avenue
articles.narrative: Prosperity Partners, an accounting and business consulting firm, has signed a 10-year lease for 24,426 square feet, encompassing the entire 11th floor, at 575 Madison Avenue in Midtown. The firm is relocating its offices within the Plaza District to this 25-story office tower. The landlord's broker noted that the building's prime location was a key factor in the tenant's decision after evaluating various submarkets.

data_points.address: 575 Madison Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.project_notes: Asking rents in the building range from $75 per square foot on lower floors to $89 per square foot on upper floors.

articles.title: The Dawn of Y’all Street: What the Texas Stock Exchange Means for Big D
articles.narrative: The Texas Stock Exchange (TXSE), approved by the SEC in 2025 and backed by over $270 million from major financial institutions, is poised to challenge the NYSE-Nasdaq duopoly. This development is expected to significantly bolster Dallas-Fort Worth's (DFW) status as a leading financial and economic hub, reinforcing a broader shift of capital and talent to North Texas. While the TXSE itself will create a modest number of direct jobs, its primary impact will be the extensive ecosystem of legal, accounting, compliance, and advisory firms that will cluster around it, driving substantial indirect job creation. This increased financial activity and job growth will fuel in-migration and, coupled with high mortgage rates and rising homeownership costs, will translate into sustained demand for multifamily housing in the DFW market.

articles.title: KCC Underway on 266,788 SF Industrial Build-Out in Hutchins, Texas
articles.narrative: Krusinski Construction Co. is currently undertaking a 266,788-square-foot tenant build-out for an HVAC products and systems provider at the Wintergreen Distribution Center in Hutchins, Texas. The industrial project, delivered in 2024 by IAC Properties, is expected to be completed in June.

data_points.notable_features: Features 40-foot clear heights, four drive-in doors, and parking for 328 cars and 151 trailers.

articles.title: Lee & Associates Negotiates Sale of Four Industrial Outdoor Storage Sites in Texas
articles.narrative: Lee & Associates has brokered the sale of a four-property industrial outdoor storage (IOS) portfolio across Texas, totaling 56.2 acres and 230,000 square feet. The properties are located in Dallas, Houston, San Antonio, and Austin. Transport Properties acquired the portfolio, which also included an additional site in Chicago, though the seller and sale price were not disclosed.

data_points.project_notes: Portfolio included an additional industrial outdoor storage facility in Chicago.

articles.title: Pinecroft Breaks Ground on $16.2M Healthcare Project in Shenandoah, Texas
articles.narrative: Pinecroft has commenced construction on a $16.2 million medical office building in Shenandoah, Texas, north of Houston. The 39,883-square-foot facility is fully preleased to various specialty healthcare providers, with completion anticipated in early 2027.

data_points.notable_features: Fully preleased to specialty healthcare providers.

articles.title: Foss Real Estate Arranges $7M in Bridge Financing for Arlington Industrial Property
articles.narrative: Foss Real Estate Partners has arranged $7 million in bridge financing for Oakhollow Business Park, an 84,469-square-foot industrial property in Arlington, Texas. The loan was structured with a 70 percent loan-to-cost ratio and a fixed interest rate of 6.5 percent. The property was built in 1984 and renovated in 2020.

data_points.notable_features: Renovated in 2020.
data_points.project_notes: Loan structured with a 70 percent loan-to-cost ratio and a fixed interest rate of 6.5 percent.

articles.title: Chicago’s cold storage reset: Modern product rises as aging inventory lags
articles.narrative: Chicago's cold storage market is undergoing a significant structural reset, with modern, purpose-built facilities outperforming an aging inventory. Despite a rise in overall vacancy to 7.6% in Q1 2026 and a 65% drop in construction activity, demand for state-of-the-art cold storage remains strong, evidenced by tight vacancy rates below 1% for modern product and a 48% increase in net absorption over the past year. This divergence is driven by tenants seeking automation-ready, energy-efficient, and compliance-forward facilities, leading to a migration away from older, less sophisticated spaces. While rental rates are stagnant due to operator economics and an oversupply of pallet positions, Chicago's fundamental advantages, such as limited land and development constraints, continue to attract institutional capital, particularly for build-to-suit projects with long-term leases.

data_points.notable_features: 15,580-pallet-position freezer with 50-foot clear heights and 14 exterior docks.

people.name: John Basile
people.title: Executive Vice President of Industrial Services

articles.title: Cawley Commercial Real Estate closes 244,343-square-foot industrial lease in Joliet
articles.narrative: Cawley Commercial Real Estate facilitated a 244,343-square-foot industrial lease for Coda Resources at a newly constructed facility in Joliet, Illinois. The long-term lease, spanning 75 months, provides the tenant with a modern industrial space featuring intermodal access, 50 dock doors, and 36-foot clear height, supporting the company's operational growth and efficiency. The tenant is expected to occupy the space in September 2026.

data_points.notable_features: Newly constructed facility with intermodal access, 50 dock doors, and 36-foot clear height.
data_points.project_notes: The tenant is expected to take occupancy in September 2026.

articles.title: Lee & Associates names VP in Rosemont office
Entre Commercial Realty closes sale of 28,300-square-foot industrial building in Wauconda
articles.narrative: Entre Commercial Realty has facilitated the sale of a 28,300-square-foot industrial building in Wauconda, Illinois. The purchaser, WSI Machinery, plans extensive renovations to both the office and warehouse areas to accommodate its ongoing growth and operational needs. The acquisition also provides future expansion potential through the leasing of a secondary unit.

data_points.notable_features: Free-standing facility with 19-foot clear height, two exterior docks, two drive-in doors (12’ x 14’), and a triple catch basin system.

articles.title: Nuveen adds to Tempe office-to-industrial pipeline with 300K sf redevelopment plans
articles.narrative: Nuveen Real Estate, through its affiliate USCIF Sky Harbor LLC, has acquired the 19-acre Sky Harbor Innovation Park in Tempe, AZ, for $23.2 million. The former office complex is slated for redevelopment into a three-building industrial park totaling approximately 300,000 square feet, with plans to retain about 60,000 square feet of office space. This acquisition and planned conversion highlight a growing trend in metro Phoenix where obsolete office properties are being redeveloped into industrial and logistics facilities, driven by strong demand for such space and the area's strategic location near the airport and major freeways.

data_points.notable_features: Located near Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport with excellent freeway access.
data_points.project_notes: The site was previously an office complex. The redevelopment plans include three industrial buildings, two of 109,870 SF and one of 78,500 SF, with approximately 60,000 SF of office space maintained. The project received administrative approval from the Tempe Development Review Commission in September.

articles.market_intelligence: First-quarter office vacancy in metro Phoenix fell to 23.5 percent from 24.5 percent quarter over quarter. Since 2020, over 8 million square feet of office space in the region has been designated for conversion, with nearly 5.7 million square feet earmarked for industrial redevelopment.

articles.title: Wall Street crowds into niche corner of real estate private credit
articles.narrative: Wall Street firms are increasingly launching non-traded mortgage REITs to tap into the growing demand for private credit in real estate. These semi-liquid vehicles, which raise capital from wealthy individual investors to finance real estate loans, mirror structures common in corporate private credit. The rapid emergence of these REITs, with seven of the top ten in fundraising launched in the past two years, indicates a burgeoning and competitive market. This shift is driven by an improving real estate picture and potential capital reallocation from corporate private credit, positioning mortgage REITs favorably as interest rates fluctuate.

articles.title: NYC’s top deals: Hennick Group buys Midtown property for $66M
articles.narrative: Hennick Group, a Toronto-based firm, acquired a 14-story commercial building at 13 West 56th Street in Midtown, New York City, for $66.2 million. The nearly 55,000-square-foot property was sold by an LLC associated with BD Hotels. This transaction represents the top commercial deal recorded in the city during the reporting period.

data_points.address: 13 West 56th Street, New York City, NY
data_points.project_notes: Property last sold in 1998.

articles.title: PolicyPro: City Council revives COPA with “marginal changes”
articles.narrative: The New York City Council has reintroduced a revised version of the Community Opportunity to Purchase Act (COPA), a bill designed to give city-approved nonprofits and their joint ventures the first right to purchase distressed multifamily buildings or properties with expiring affordability restrictions. The updated bill, championed by Council member Sandy Nurse, aims to be more targeted by narrowing eligibility criteria for properties and shortening procedural timelines for nonprofits to express interest and make offers. While some changes have been made, critics argue that the core impact of the bill, which could delay property sales, remains largely the same. The revised bill also introduces a clearer penalty structure for non-compliant owners, imposing fines of up to 15 percent of a property's sale price.

articles.title: LISTEN: The exodus of wealthy tenants at a UES mainstay
articles.narrative: The article discusses the predicament of luxury tenants at 800 Fifth Avenue on the Upper East Side, who are facing an exodus after the building was acquired by Miki Naftali for $800 million with plans to convert it into condominiums. This situation has forced these tenants into a competitive luxury rental market where prices have significantly increased, creating challenges for them and their brokers. The building's leasing office has closed, and tenants are expected to vacate by year-end.

data_points.address: 800 Fifth Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.project_notes: The property is being converted from rental to condominium use.

articles.market_intelligence: Prices for luxury rental units in the Upper East Side market have skyrocketed.

articles.title: Naftali, Blavatnik snag $374M refi for Williamsburg Wharf
articles.narrative: Naftali Group and Access Industries, led by Miki Naftali and Len Blavatnik respectively, have secured a $374 million refinancing package from Barings for the first phase of their Williamsburg Wharf master-planned development. This new financing will retire existing debt from Bank OZK. The initial phase of the project, which includes 89 condominiums, over 500 rental apartments, and 15,000 square feet of retail, has seen strong market reception, with the condo units over 70 percent sold and the rental units over 90 percent occupied.

data_points.address: 470 Kent Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Master-planned development with nearly 525 feet of water frontage, featuring a rooftop pool deck, private outdoor ice-skating rink, and an indoor-outdoor fitness center.
data_points.project_notes: The first phase includes 89 condos and over 500 apartments. The condo portion is over 70% sold, and the rental portion is over 90% occupied. The refinancing retires a previous $310 million senior loan from Bank OZK and $75 million in mezzanine financing from Barings, along with an additional $238 million loan from Bank OZK.

articles.title: Long-operating Garden State REIT calls it a day
articles.narrative: The First Real Estate Investment Trust of New Jersey (FREIT) has announced a plan for voluntary liquidation, intending to sell its entire portfolio of over a dozen properties, primarily in New Jersey, over the next 24 months. The move is expected to return significant capital to stockholders, with estimated net proceeds ranging from $24.44 to $30.03 per share, compared to a pre-announcement stock price of $15.25. The portfolio includes multifamily complexes, shopping centers, and vacant land, as well as a property in New York.

data_points.project_notes: FREIT was incorporated in 1961. The company previously had a $186 million deal with Kushner Companies for six apartment properties in northern New Jersey and the Hudson Valley, which was canceled during the pandemic, leading to a $16.9 million settlement in 2024.

companies_people.label: OWNER
companies_people.firm_name: First Real Estate Investment Trust of New Jersey (FREIT)

articles.title: South Florida’s top deals: McDonald’s franchisee sells Palm Beach estate for $37M
articles.narrative: A waterfront estate in Palm Beach, Florida, was sold for $37.1 million by Violet Hill Development Company Ltd., an entity connected to the late McDonald's franchisee George Cohen. The 8,600-square-foot mansion, featuring six bedrooms and seven and a half bathrooms, was acquired by Elland Properties LLC. This transaction represents the top home sale recorded in South Florida for the period.

articles.title: Industrial and dev sites drive South Florida i-sales boom, but party’s over for multifamily
articles.narrative: Investment sales activity in South Florida surged by nearly 30% year-over-year in the first quarter, reaching $4.3 billion across 244 transactions, according to an Avison Young report. This growth was primarily driven by a 102.4% increase in industrial deal volume, totaling $1.3 billion, and a 123% jump in development site sales, which hit $503.9 million. Retail and office sectors also saw increases, while multifamily transactions experienced a notable decline after several years of strong performance, attributed to a slowdown in new resident influx and a surge in new unit completions.

articles.title: Golden Beach broker lands $48M verdict in six-year fight over stiffed commission
articles.narrative: A jury awarded real estate broker Alexander Goldstein $47.8 million in damages, including punitive and compensatory damages, in a six-year legal battle over a stiffed commission. Goldstein sued Reuben Ezekiel, alleging fraud and tortious interference after Ezekiel purchased a waterfront home for $2.8 million, a deal Goldstein had negotiated, but then used his sister as a straw broker to collect the commission. The verdict, which includes significant punitive damages, aims to deter such behavior in the real estate industry.

data_points.address: 313 Center Island Drive, Golden Beach, FL
data_points.notable_features: Waterfront home.

articles.title: Rockstar Energy billionaire finds new buyer for waterfront North Bay Road home
articles.narrative: Billionaire Russell Savage has found a new buyer for his waterfront home at 5300 North Bay Road in Miami Beach, which was gut-renovated by Keith Menin. The property, asking $28 million, leads the 22 luxury home contracts signed in Miami-Dade County between May 11 and May 17. This transaction highlights the continued activity in Miami-Dade's luxury residential market, where properties spent an average of 176 days on the market.

data_points.address: 5300 North Bay Road, Miami Beach, FL
data_points.notable_features: Waterfront property with a four-bedroom main house, a detached three-bedroom guest house, garage, Jacuzzi, pool, and outdoor kitchen.
data_points.project_notes: Property was gut-renovated by Menin Homes.

articles.market_intelligence: The Miami-Dade luxury home market saw 22 contracts signed between May 11 and May 17, with properties spending an average of 176 days on the market. The total asking dollar volume for these contracts was $238.3 million. In New York, 38 luxury home contracts were signed in the same period, with a combined asking price of $273.5 million and an average of 604 days on the market.

articles.title: “Perception becomes reality”: Miami-Dade office rents enter new era at over $200 psf
articles.narrative: Miami-Dade County's prime office market is experiencing a significant surge in rents, with some deals now surpassing $200 per square foot, a substantial increase from the $100 per square foot records set during the pandemic. This escalation is largely driven by the continued influx of high-net-worth individuals and their associated firms, particularly from out-of-state, seeking bespoke office spaces near their residences. While this trend primarily impacts boutique, high-end buildings and does not reflect the broader market's occupancy or lease rates, it signifies a new era for luxury office properties in the region, with developers responding by offering turnkey spaces and generous tenant allowances.

articles.title: Houston-based Hines scoops up Chicago shopping center for $70M
articles.narrative: Hines Global Income Trust has acquired the Wicker Park Commons shopping center in Chicago from DWS Group for $70 million. The 218,000-square-foot retail property, anchored by a grocery store and a home improvement retailer, was purchased at a significant discount from its 2015 sale price. This acquisition marks Hines' second-largest in Chicago in recent months, reflecting their continued investment in the market.

data_points.notable_features: Anchored by a grocery store and a home improvement retailer, with additional retail and 140,000 square feet of office space.
data_points.project_notes: DWS Group acquired the property for $94.75 million in 2015. The home improvement retailer's lease was initiated in 2015 for 15 years with multiple 5-year extension options.

articles.title: Chicago MLS goes nuclear: MRED plans to sever Zillow feed
articles.narrative: Midwest Real Estate Data (MRED), the MLS operator for northern Illinois, plans to suspend its data feed to Zillow if the home search giant does not comply with MRED's rules regarding the display of listings marketed privately before public release. This dispute, which has escalated into a federal lawsuit filed by Zillow, centers on Zillow's refusal to display listings pre-marketed through channels like MRED's Private Listing Network or Compass's "Private Exclusives." MRED argues that Zillow's filtering of listings based on the attached broker or brokerage violates its licensing agreement, while Zillow contends that MRED is using its market dominance to protect Compass listings nationwide. The potential data blackout would significantly reduce Zillow's listing volume in the Chicago area, impacting both sellers' exposure and buyers' access to available homes.

articles.title: Namdar’s River Oaks mall now under state microscope
articles.narrative: Calumet City is seeking state approval for a quick-takeover of the River Oaks City Mall through eminent domain, citing the owner's alleged lack of investment and the mall's deteriorating condition. The city claims the owner is merely holding onto the property for current gains rather than investing in its future. This action follows a dismissed lawsuit by the owner against the city, which alleged tenant losses due to a city letter about potential takeover.

data_points.address: 96 River Oaks Center Drive, Calumet City, IL
data_points.notable_features: City previously appraised land parcels at $4.6 million and offered to purchase for $13.2 million; owner countered for over $40 million.
data_points.project_notes: City filed a petition with the Cook County court for eminent domain of the site. Owner previously filed a lawsuit against the city, which was dismissed.

articles.title: Hines scores $142M refi for Wolf Point West rental tower
articles.narrative: Hines and its partners have secured a $141.8 million refinancing for Wolf Point West, a 509-unit luxury rental tower located on the Chicago River. The three-year, floating-rate loan was provided by New York Life Insurance Company and arranged by JLL Capital Markets. This transaction marks the second significant debt deal for the ownership group at the riverfront site within the past year, reflecting strong institutional investor interest in Chicago's multifamily market due to limited new supply and robust rent growth.

data_points.address: 343 West Wolf Point Plaza, Chicago, IL
data_points.notable_features: Luxury rental tower located on a riverfront site at the confluence of the three branches of the Chicago River.
data_points.project_notes: The new loan retires a previous $142.5 million mortgage from Teachers Annuity Association of America, taken out in 2016. The tower cost $160 million to build.

articles.market_intelligence: Chicago rents are growing at 4.2 percent annually, significantly higher than the 0.4 percent national average. Chicago continues to be a major destination for corporate relocation. The owners of the luxury Aqua tower in Lakeshore East secured a $144 million refinancing package in April.

articles.title: Carter Cadillac of Chicago set to open in 2027
articles.narrative: Jack Carter Automotive Group is developing the first Cadillac dealership within Chicago city limits, located at 1400 West North Avenue. The group acquired the 2.2-acre site for $15.5 million from Sterling Bay, with an anticipated opening in late 2027. The property was originally part of Sterling Bay's ambitious Lincoln Yards megaproject, which faced significant challenges and ultimately saw much of its land returned to lenders and sold off.

data_points.notable_features: First individual Cadillac dealership within Chicago city limits.
data_points.project_notes: Site was previously part of Sterling Bay's Lincoln Yards megaproject and was acquired by Sterling Bay seven years prior to this transaction. Sterling Bay had previously envisioned a 100,000-square-foot medical office building for the site.

articles.title: Life after IBM: Brandywine tees up $31M in renovations for 66-acre Austin office campus
articles.narrative: Brandywine Realty Trust is undertaking a $31 million renovation of Building 902 at its 66-acre Uptown ATX campus in North Austin, following IBM's planned departure in mid-2027. The project, which includes interior and exterior upgrades to the 172,000-square-foot office building, is expected to begin by July 2026 and conclude by the end of 2027. This renovation marks a strategic shift for Brandywine, which initially planned to demolish existing structures at the former Broadmoor campus but has pivoted to extensive renovations across three buildings totaling approximately 500,000 square feet, reflecting the softening Austin office market.

data_points.notable_features: Interior and exterior renovation including revamped lobby, bathrooms, and new amenities.
data_points.project_notes: Part of a larger renovation plan for buildings 902, 904, and 906, comprising about 500,000 square feet of office space. The overall Uptown ATX project was originally planned nearly a decade ago as a demolition and redevelopment, but has shifted to renovation due to changes in the Austin office market. The campus was formerly known as Broadmoor office campus.

articles.market_intelligence: Austin office market vacancy increased from 22.1% in Q4 2025 to 22.4% in Q1 2026. Overall asking rents dropped from $46.20 per square foot to $45.45 per square foot during the same period.

articles.title: Hillwood, Vanderbilt take larger bite of marquee Las Colinas office complex
articles.narrative: A joint venture between Vanderbilt Office Properties and Hillwood Urban has increased its ownership stake in the 1.4 million-square-foot Towers at Williams Square office complex in Irving, Texas. The transaction, which also brought in TriPost Capital Partners as a new equity partner, represents one of the largest office trades in Dallas-Fort Worth this year. The property, known for its prominent location in the Las Colinas master-planned community and its strong tenant mix, was financed by Beal Bank.

data_points.notable_features: Three-building office campus with two 14-story buildings and a 26-story high-rise, known for its bronze mustang statues and located in the Las Colinas master-planned community.
data_points.project_notes: The previous ownership group acquired the building for $330 million in 2015 and completed a $30 million renovation in 2024.

articles.title: Wolfe Investments slapped with another potential foreclosure
articles.narrative: The former Fort Worth Star-Telegram building at 307 West Seventh Street in Fort Worth, TX, is facing potential foreclosure as B1Bank seeks to sell its "sub-performing" $21 million loan on the property. The loan, provided to an entity tied to Wolfe Investments, matured in April 2026. This situation follows a series of financial difficulties for Wolfe Investments, including a prior foreclosure on an adjacent property and multiple lawsuits for loan defaults across its multifamily renovation portfolio.

data_points.address: 307 West Seventh Street, Fort Worth, TX
data_points.notable_features: Historic 19-story building, formerly home to the Fort Worth Star-Telegram.
data_points.project_notes: Initial plan was to convert the building into luxury apartments. The property is valued at $22 million by the Tarrant Appraisal District.

tenants: U.S. Housing and Urban Development Department

articles.title: Rubicon Point wades into Santa Clara with $87M office campus pickup
articles.narrative: Rubicon Point Partners has acquired a four-building office campus in Santa Clara, CA, for $87.2 million, marking a significant entry into the South Bay market. The 201,100-square-foot complex, fully occupied by a medical device manufacturer, was purchased at a discount to its assessed value. This acquisition represents the initial phase of Rubicon Point's planned billion-dollar investment strategy across the Bay Area.

data_points.address: 3003 Bunker Hill Lane, Santa Clara, CA
data_points.project_notes: The sale price was 15.7% less than the assessed value of $103.4 million.

articles.market_intelligence: The Bay Area office market has experienced a value tumble after a post-pandemic peak.

articles.title: Presidio Bay moves in on Mack Real Estate loan tied to historic downtown SF offices
articles.narrative: Presidio Bay Ventures is in discussions to acquire a nonperforming $100 million loan tied to the historic 301 Battery Street office building in San Francisco. The acquisition of this debt from Mack Real Estate, which originated the loan when RFR Holdings purchased the property in 2020, would position Presidio Bay to take control of the asset through foreclosure or a deed-in-lieu process. The potential deal, valued at approximately $70 million, reflects a significant discount from the property's last sale price and highlights the ongoing challenges in the downtown San Francisco office market.

data_points.notable_features: Historic Beaux-Arts property, former Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco building, with a 28,000-square-foot banking hall being marketed as a venue and events hub.
data_points.project_notes: Property was previously acquired by RFR Holdings for $143 million in 2020. The loan is expected to sell for approximately $70 million, implying a valuation of around $300-per-square-foot.

articles.title: Harbor Associates, Farallon Acquire Yorba Linda Industrial Campus Off-Market
articles.narrative: Harbor Associates and Farallon Capital Management have acquired the Yorba Linda Commerce Center, a 280,000-square-foot industrial campus in Yorba Linda, CA, for $81 million in an off-market transaction. The property, which was 98% leased at closing, offers local businesses a high-quality, irreplaceable location with a mix of suite sizes to support current and future operational needs. The acquisition was financed with a $56.3 million loan from an affiliate of QuadReal Property Group.

data_points.notable_features: Small bay, multi-tenant industrial campus.
data_points.project_notes: Property was owned by a private family for over 30 years.

articles.title: Haussmann Development Closes on Construction Loan for Manhattanville Mixed-Income
articles.narrative: Haussmann Development secured a $40.75 million construction loan from Affinius Capital and Axonic Capital for an 84-unit mixed-income multifamily building at 16-20 Convent Ave. in Manhattanville. The project, located in an Opportunity Zone near Columbia University, will provide permanently affordable housing units under New York City's 485-x tax abatement program and utilize the Universal Affordability Preference density bonus. This marks Haussmann Development's fifth project in Northern Manhattan.

data_points.address: 16–20 Convent Ave., New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Located in an Opportunity Zone adjacent to Columbia University's expanding campus; designed to provide permanently affordable housing units.
data_points.project_notes: Developed pursuant to New York City’s 485-x tax abatement program and utilizing the Universal Affordability Preference (UAP) density bonus.

articles.title: Upland Apartments’ Original Developer Divests for $48M
articles.narrative: The original developer of Upland Village Green, a 24-building multifamily property in Upland, CA, has divested the asset for $48.5 million. The sale, brokered by Institutional Property Advisors, marks the first time the property has traded in over 40 years. The buyer, WMC Commercial Properties, is positioned to benefit from strong cash flow and opportunities for value-add enhancements and operational efficiencies.

data_points.notable_features: 24-building multifamily property located on the eastern border of Upland near Rancho Cucamonga and Ontario.
data_points.project_notes: Property was placed on the market for the first time in over 40 years by the original developer and operator.

articles.market_intelligence: The sale represents one of only four 100-plus unit value-add transactions in San Bernardino County in the past 24 months.

articles.title: Small Colleges Are Dying. Developers See Opportunity
articles.narrative: The closure of small colleges across the U.S. is creating significant redevelopment opportunities for real estate developers and larger educational institutions, as nearly 300 colleges closed between 2008 and 2023, with hundreds more projected to shutter. These vacated campuses, often comprising thousands of acres with residential, academic, and faculty buildings, are being eyed for adaptive reuse into housing, office, or community services, or acquired by well-endowed universities seeking expansion. While some campuses face challenges due to remote locations or deferred maintenance, others in desirable areas are attracting buyers, though local political pushback can sometimes complicate redevelopment plans.

people.name: David Carlos
people.title: Head of Nonprofit, Education & Government Practice for the Tri-State Area

articles.title: Records Hint Dilweg Is Behind On Loan For Landmark Atlanta Skyscraper
articles.narrative: The Dilweg Co. appears to be significantly behind on its mortgage for 101 Marietta St., a 36-story office tower in Downtown Atlanta, with an outstanding balance of $68.5M as of March 2025. The loan, originally $64.5M from Citizens Bank in 2015, matured in August 2025 and has not been satisfied or extended according to public records. The property, which Dilweg had previously marketed for sale, has since been pulled from the market, reflecting the challenging conditions in the Downtown Atlanta office sector.

data_points.notable_features: Distinctive chevron-shaped roof; headquarters office for the Atlanta Hawks.
data_points.project_notes: Dilweg Co. purchased the tower in 2015 for $68.8M. The loan was modified five times. Dilweg invested approximately $12M into the property since 2016 and completed 310K SF of leasing activity over the past six years. Fulton County valued the property at $79.6M in 2025, down from $85.4M in 2023.

articles.market_intelligence: Downtown Atlanta's office market experienced a vacancy rate exceeding 30% in Q1 2026, with 124K SF of negative absorption. In contrast, Midtown Atlanta's office submarket had a nearly 30% vacancy rate but saw over 300K SF of leasing activity in the same quarter. The $80M loan for 191 Peachtree Tower was placed on a watchlist last month by Morningstar Credit, and Fitch Ratings downgraded a Morgan Stanley CMBS pool partly due to this loan.

articles.title: Value Of Tishman Speyer's Long Island City Office Complex Slashed In Loan Workout
articles.narrative: Tishman Speyer has secured a three-year extension on the $425 million CMBS loan for The Jacx, a 1.2 million-square-foot office complex in Long Island City, New York. The extension, which includes a 1.5-year option, was granted after Tishman Speyer injected an additional $20 million in equity. The property's appraised value has been significantly reduced to $480 million, a 44% decrease from its 2021 valuation, reflecting challenges such as a major vacancy from a large tenant that never occupied its space and another tenant's lease abandonment. Despite these hurdles, Tishman Speyer expressed confidence in the quality of the development and the surrounding neighborhood.

data_points.notable_features: Two 26-story towers.
data_points.project_notes: The property was appraised at $480M, down 44% from its 2021 appraised value of $860M. Tishman Speyer injected $20M of equity for the loan extension. The loan was sent to special servicing in February 2026. The deal is subject to passing debt yield hurdles and will return to the master servicer after three months of performance.

articles.title: Coffee, Chicken And Clinics Fuel Chicago's Post-Covid Resurgence
articles.narrative: Chicago's retail sector has experienced a significant post-pandemic recovery, largely fueled by the robust performance of quick-service restaurants, particularly coffee and chicken concepts, and medical retail. Panelists at a recent summit highlighted a dramatic shift from the challenging conditions during the pandemic, with key shopping corridors now exhibiting tight vacancy rates and a highly competitive leasing environment. Brokers note the difficulty in securing space for new tenants in popular areas due to limited availability and exclusive landlord deals, while also observing a strong entrepreneurial drive among prospective local tenants seeking discounted sublease opportunities.

articles.title: Landlords Agree To Pay $218M To Settle Price-Fixing Claims In RealPage Antitrust Case
articles.narrative: Eleven major real estate firms have agreed to pay a combined $218 million to settle price-fixing claims in an antitrust class-action lawsuit against RealPage. The settlement, filed in the U.S. District Court for the Middle District of Tennessee, requires the landlords to cease using nonpublic rental data from RealPage's rent-setting software, formerly known as Yieldstar, and to stop using the software to set prices for their rental apartments. This agreement follows a previous $141.8 million settlement with 27 other firms and a separate settlement by RealPage with federal prosecutors, which mandated changes to its software's rent suggestion methods.
